description:
## Refactor

`src/fetcher/refresh_loop.cr` is **908 lines** combining 6+ unrelated concerns. The file should be split so each concern lives in its own file with a single, clear responsibility.

Per the maintainability principle: **if two pieces of code are not about the same thing, they should not live in the same file.** Today they are forced to share a file because they share a module, but that coupling is incidental, not logical.

## Proposed file split

| New file | Contents | Approx LOC |
|---|---|---|
| `src/fetcher/refresh_loop.cr` | `RefreshLoop` module: fetch orchestration, supervisor loop, `State`, top-level shims (the public API contract) | ~300 |
| `src/fetcher/concurrency/semaphore.cr` | `RefreshLoop::Semaphore` (or top-level `FeedFetchSemaphore`): `CONCURRENCY_LIMIT`, `acquire_semaphore`, `release_semaphore`, `semaphore_health_status`, `reset_semaphore`, `check_semaphore_health` | ~80 |
| `src/fetcher/refresh_health_monitor.cr` | `RefreshLoop::RefreshHealthMonitor` module: cycle start/complete/failure tracking, `stuck?`, `attempt_recovery`, `force_stuck!` | ~110 |
| `src/services/gc_collector.cr` | `RefreshLoop::GCCollector` module (or new top-level `GCCollector`): incremental + periodic full GC, `stats` | ~50 |
| `src/services/fiber_tracker.cr` | `RefreshLoop::FiberTracker` module: active/peak/spawn counters, `stats`, `reset` | ~40 |

## Why per-file

- `GCCollector` and `FiberTracker` are **observability primitives** — they could be reused by other code paths (e.g., the websocket manager, the admin controller). Keeping them trapped inside `RefreshLoop` makes that reuse impossible without circular imports.
- `RefreshHealthMonitor` already has public callers (`admin_controller.cr`, `api_base_controller.cr`) — moving it to its own file makes the public surface visible and stable.
- `Semaphore` could be replaced with a shared `src/concurrency/feed_fetch_semaphore.cr` if the project wants a general-purpose fetch semaphore for other fetchers (favicon, software releases).

## Order of operations

This ticket is **blocked by**:

- `DRY-1` — extract `fallback_feed` helper first; this is easier after the helper exists.
- `DRY-2` — extract `interruptible_sleep` helper first; same reason.
- All `REL-*` tickets — reliability bugs should be fixed first so the split is into a known-good base.

Once the helpers exist and bugs are fixed, the file split is mechanical.

## Acceptance

- Each of the 5 files above exists and is independently `require`d by `src/quickheadlines.cr` (or wherever the entry point is).
- `wc -l` on the new `src/fetcher/refresh_loop.cr` is **≤ 350** (down from 908).
- `wc -l` on each new file matches the table above (±20%).
- All existing call sites of `RefreshHealthMonitor`, `GCCollector`, `FiberTracker` still work without changes (the module is re-exported via `alias` if needed for back-compat, or callers are updated in this same change).
- `just nix-build` passes; existing Crystal specs pass; manual smoke test passes.
- The mermaid dependency graph in the parent epic shows this as the final structural change.
title: Refactor: split refresh_loop.cr into 5 focused files (semaphore, health, GC, fiber, loop)
